name: Sinbad.io (Treasury hash)
notes: OFAC SANCTIONED Nov-2023. Successor to Blender.io. Used by Lazarus Group to launder $100M Atomic Wallet exploit funds.

name: Railgun Privacy Pool (USDC)
notes: Used in Jun-2023 Atomic Wallet exploit laundering. Not OFAC-sanctioned but flagged by FBI as obfuscation infrastructure.

name: Railgun Privacy Pool (ETH)
notes: Privacy protocol contract address. Confidence is medium because contract layout changes between Railgun versions; verify via tx-level routing.

name: FixedFloat (non-KYC swap)
notes: Non-KYC cross-chain swap service routinely used in laundering flows. Not OFAC-sanctioned but high-risk pattern.

name: RAILGUN: Relay Contract (Ethereum)
notes: Canonical RAILGUN Relay proxy on Ethereum mainnet (PausableUpgradableProxy, EIP-1967). Routes shielded zk-SNARK transactions for the RAILGUN privacy system. Not OFAC-sanctioned but $2B+ in cumulative shielded volume — high investigative priority.

name: RAILGUN: Smart Wallet (Ethereum)
notes: RAILGUN Smart Wallet contract on Ethereum mainnet — handles shield/unshield deposits and private-transaction processing. Core privacy-pool entry/exit point alongside the Relay contract. zk-SNARK based, multi-asset (ETH, ERC-20).

name: RAILGUN: Treasury (Ethereum)
notes: RAILGUN protocol treasury contract — collects shielded-transaction fees in RAIL token. Not a deposit/withdraw venue itself but co-occurrence in a flow signals RAILGUN-protocol context.

name: RAILGUN: Smart Wallet Implementation (multi-chain)
notes: EIP-1967 implementation contract behind the RAILGUN Relay proxies on Ethereum, BSC, Polygon, and Arbitrum. Same bytecode deployed across all four chains — flags the deployer pattern.

name: RAILGUN: Relay Contract (BSC)
notes: RAILGUN Relay proxy on BNB Smart Chain — BSC deployment of the privacy system. Used in cross-chain laundering flows where BSC stablecoin liquidity routes through shielded pools.

name: RAILGUN: Relay Contract (Polygon)
notes: RAILGUN Relay proxy on Polygon PoS — Polygon deployment of the shielded-pool privacy system. Low-fee chain attracts higher tx-count laundering patterns vs. Ethereum mainnet.

name: RAILGUN: Relay Contract (Arbitrum)
notes: RAILGUN Relay proxy on Arbitrum One — same address as Ethereum mainnet (deterministic deploy). Distinct deployment per Arbiscan. Privacy is anchored to RAIL token staked on Ethereum L1.
_audit_status: externally_verified_v031: WebFetch from https://arbiscan.io/address/0xfa7093cdd9ee6932b4eb2c9e1cde7ce00b1fa4b9 (Arbiscan label 'PausableUpgradableProxy', confirmed RAILGUN)

name: RAILGUN: RelayAdapt (Arbitrum)
notes: RAILGUN RelayAdapt on Arbitrum — composability adapter that lets shielded RAILGUN funds interact with external Arbitrum DeFi contracts in a single shielded tx. Critical for tracing fund-flow into shielded set.

name: Aztec: Connect (Ethereum)
notes: Aztec Connect L1 contract — privacy-preserving zkRollup escrow. Aztec Connect shut down Mar-2024 (ownership renounced, deposits disabled) but $2.7M+ residual escrow remains. Withdrawals via Aztec Connect Ejector. Historical mixer-tier privacy hop.

name: Aztec: Private Rollup Bridge (Ethereum)
notes: Aztec 2.0 Private Rollup L1 bridge — older zk-zk-rollup shielded-pool entry/exit predating Aztec Connect. zkETH/zkDAI minted via this bridge. Funds entering or leaving via this contract pass through Aztec's shielded set.
_audit_status: externally_verified_v031: WebFetch from https://etherscan.io/address/0x737901bea3eeb88459df9ef1be8ff3ae1b42a2ba (Etherscan label 'Aztec: Private Rollup Bridge')

name: Aztec: Connect Rollup Deployment (Ethereum)
notes: Aztec Connect initial deployment contract (deployed Jun-2022). Documented in Aztec's own HackMD deployment-transactions log. Medium confidence because this was a v1 deploy that was subsequently superseded; verify tx-level routing against the active Connect address.

name: Aztec: Connect Verification Key (Ethereum)
notes: Aztec Connect verification-key contract — holds the zkSNARK verifier used by the Aztec Connect rollup. Not a deposit venue but co-occurrence in a tx graph signals Aztec-Connect-proven flow.
_audit_status: externally_verified_v031: WebFetch from https://etherscan.io/address/0x8C3B53F450E53FF14D8C9449465F79ff40668966 (Etherscan label 'Aztec: Aztec Connect Verification Key')

name: Aztec: Fee Distributor (Ethereum)
notes: Aztec Fee Distributor — collects rollup fees. Same caveat as the Verification Key: not a deposit venue but a strong co-signal for Aztec-protocol context in a fund-flow graph.
_audit_status: externally_verified_v031: WebFetch from https://etherscan.io/address/0x4cf32670a53657596E641DFCC6d40f01e4d64927 (Etherscan label 'Aztec: Aztec Fee Distributor')

name: Privacy Pools (0xbow): Entrypoint (Ethereum)
notes: 0xbow Privacy Pools Entrypoint on Ethereum mainnet — main router users interact with (March 2025 launch). 'Compliant' privacy via Association Set Provider that screens deposits with KYT. Vitalik-backed; $6M+ volume by mid-2025.

name: Privacy Pools (0xbow): ETH Pool (Ethereum)
notes: 0xbow Privacy Pools ETH pool contract — per-asset pool holding deposited ETH and the Merkle tree of commitments. Separate pools exist for USDC/USDT/DAI/wBTC. Withdrawals use zk-proofs of association-set membership.

name: Nocturne: Teller (Ethereum)
notes: Nocturne Teller contract on Ethereum mainnet — main entry point for the shielded-pool. Backed by Vitalik/Polychain/Bain Capital. Status: project wound down post-2024 but contract retains funds; verify activity before flagging.

name: Nocturne: Handler (Ethereum)
notes: Nocturne Handler contract — executes shielded operations against the canonical shielded pool. Co-occurs with Teller in any Nocturne tx. Stealth-address + shielded-pool hybrid design.

name: Nocturne: DepositManager (Ethereum)
notes: Nocturne DepositManager — accepts and screens deposits before they enter the shielded set. Implements a deposit-rate-limit compliance layer (a la Privacy Pools' ASP). Tracing the perp's funds INTO this address means they shielded.
